data.title: South China Vocational Education Group Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Results and Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

South China Vocational Education Group Co., Ltd. ( (HK:6913) ) just unveiled an update.

South China Vocational Education Group Company Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 28 May 2026 in Guangzhou, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the accompanying board and auditor reports. The meeting will also consider re-electing one executive and two independent non-executive directors, reappointing AOGB CPA Limited as auditor, and granting the board a refreshed mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, a move that preserves financing flexibility and could influence future capital-raising plans and governance continuity.

If approved, the director re-elections and auditor reappointment would maintain management and oversight stability at a time when regulatory compliance and educational quality are crucial for listed vocational providers in China. The proposed general mandate for new share issuance, excluding rights issues, employee schemes, and certain existing instruments, would give the board broad but time-limited authority to tap equity markets, potentially affecting shareholder dilution, capital structure, and the company’s capacity to fund expansion or strategic initiatives in the competitive vocational education sector.

More about South China Vocational Education Group Co., Ltd.

South China Vocational Education Group Company Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated education provider listed in Hong Kong, focusing on vocational education services in South China. The group operates through subsidiaries and is subject to the Listing Rules of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, reflecting its positioning within the regulated higher education and training sector.

Average Trading Volume: 1,045,285

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$273.5M

data.title: Shanghai Electric Tightens Governance With New Remuneration Committee Procedures
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shanghai Electric Group Company ( (HK:2727) ) has issued an announcement.

Shanghai Electric Group Company Limited has approved updated rules of procedure for its Board’s Remuneration Committee, defining its role in designing transparent remuneration policies and structures for directors and senior management. The rules specify the committee’s composition, requiring at least three directors with a majority being independent non-executive directors, and mandate that the chair must also be an independent non-executive director.

The procedures set out detailed requirements for meetings, including quorum thresholds, voting rules, notice periods, acceptable formats such as in-person or virtual participation, and proxy attendance conditions. The company also formalized the role of the secretary in recording deliberations and dissenting opinions, signaling a stronger focus on governance transparency, accountability, and standardized oversight of executive pay decisions.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2727) stock is a Buy with a HK$4.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shanghai Electric Group Company stock, see the HK:2727 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shanghai Electric Group Company

Shanghai Electric Group Company Limited is a Chinese industrial group operating in the equipment manufacturing and engineering sector. The company is overseen by a board of directors and employs formal board committees and procedural rules to govern directors and senior management, reflecting an emphasis on corporate governance and regulatory compliance.

data.title: HEG clears FY26 results, dividend and governance moves, backs TACC funding
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from HEG Limited ( (IN:HEG) ).

HEG Limited’s board has approved the audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended 31 March 2026, alongside cash flow statements and statements of assets and liabilities, all supported by unmodified audit opinions. The board also recommended a final dividend of Rs. 3.40 per equity share for FY 2025-26, underscoring continued shareholder returns.

The company cleared the continuation of Shri Shekhar Agarwal as a non-executive, non-independent director beyond his 75th birthday, subject to shareholder approval, reinforcing board continuity and confirming he faces no regulatory debarment. In addition, HEG reappointed its cost, internal and tax auditors for the next financial cycle and authorised the creation of security in favour of lenders for credit facilities to its wholly owned subsidiary TACC Limited, signaling ongoing support for group financing and operational expansion.

More about HEG Limited

HEG Limited is an India-based manufacturer in the graphite electrodes and carbon products space, serving steelmakers and related heavy industries in global markets. The company operates through a network of plants and offices in Madhya Pradesh and the NCR region, aligning its governance and audit practices with Indian securities regulations and international quality standards.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Merlin Announces $80 Million Private Equity Investment Agreement
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Merlin ( (MRLN) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 29, 2026, Merlin, Inc. announced it had entered into a private investment in public equity agreement with an existing institutional shareholder to raise $80 million through the sale of 8 million common shares at $10 each and warrants for 4 million additional shares. The transaction, structured as a private placement exempt from U.S. securities registration requirements, is expected to close around May 1, 2026 and will trigger anti-dilution adjustments to the company’s existing convertible preferred stock and certain warrants.

The financing will lift Merlin’s total cash resources to about $183 million and is intended to accelerate revenue generation and long-term value creation, including advancing its core autonomous flight platform, funding regulatory approvals and scaling program capacity. The updated registration rights arrangements commit Merlin to register the resale of the new shares and warrant shares, bolstering liquidity for investors, while the investment deepens the relationship with a long-term shareholder and reinforces Merlin’s balance sheet as it pursues 2026–2027 program milestones and its commercial launch timeline.

The most recent analyst rating on (MRLN) stock is a Buy with a $25.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Merlin stock, see the MRLN Stock Forecast page.

More about Merlin

Merlin, Inc. is a U.S.-based aerospace technology company specializing in cost-effective, takeoff-to-touchdown autonomous flight systems for both legacy and next-generation aircraft. Its aircraft-agnostic, AI-powered software serves military and civil programs, supporting a growing range of missions and platforms and backed by more than $100 million in awarded military contracts.

The company focuses on assured, autonomous flight technology that addresses national security challenges while enabling commercial applications. Its systems have been validated through hundreds of autonomous flights at test facilities worldwide, positioning Merlin as a leading player in defense-related autonomy and emerging commercial autonomous aviation markets.

Average Trading Volume: 855,193

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $289.8M

3i Infrastructure ( (GB:3IN) ) has shared an announcement.

3i Infrastructure plc, a Jersey-incorporated and London-listed investment trust, focuses on responsible infrastructure investments that generate sustainable long-term returns for shareholders and support positive outcomes for portfolio companies. The company is managed by 3i Investments plc, a subsidiary of 3i Group plc authorised and regulated by the UK Financial Conduct Authority.

The company has appointed Andrew Sykes as Chair Designate to succeed Richard Laing, with Sykes joining the board as an independent non-executive director on 23 July 2026 and becoming chair on 1 January 2027 after a handover period. With extensive experience across investment trusts, infrastructure and financial services, Sykes is expected to strengthen the board’s expertise and support the execution of 3i Infrastructure’s strategy, while Laing provides continuity until his departure and Sykes steps down from the ICG plc board in 2027.

data.title: China Railway Group First-Quarter Profit Drops as Revenue and Cash Flow Weaken
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Railway Group ( (HK:0390) ) has issued an announcement.

China Railway Group reported a weak first quarter for 2026, with revenue falling 5.46% year-on-year to RMB 235.0 billion and profit before tax down 19.64%. Net profit attributable to shareholders dropped 27.65% to RMB 4.36 billion, while earnings per share declined in tandem and net profit excluding non-recurring items fell almost 30%, underscoring pressure on underlying profitability.

Operating cash flow remained deeply negative at RMB -86.43 billion, widening from the prior year’s outflow and highlighting ongoing working capital strain in the group’s project-heavy business model. Total assets edged down 1.86% from year-end 2025, though owners’ equity attributable to shareholders rose modestly by 0.87%, suggesting some balance-sheet resilience despite the earnings deterioration.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0390) stock is a Buy with a HK$5.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Railway Group stock, see the HK:0390 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Railway Group

China Railway Group Limited is a major Chinese infrastructure and construction conglomerate focused on railway and transportation engineering projects. The company operates across large-scale civil engineering, construction contracting and related services, positioning it as a key player in China’s transport infrastructure development and associated capital projects.

data.title: Productive Technologies Launches Equity Incentive Plan at PDT Xuzhou, Grants Options to Key Executive
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

IDG Energy Investment Limited ( (HK:0650) ) has issued an announcement.

Productive Technologies Company Limited has approved an equity incentive plan at its subsidiary PDT Xuzhou to grant equity-linked options to directors, senior management and core staff, allowing them to acquire up to 15% of the unit’s enlarged registered capital through a dedicated shareholding platform. The move is designed to deepen employee commitment and attract talent, and is treated as a deemed disposal under Hong Kong listing rules, though it falls below thresholds that would trigger a discloseable transaction.

Under the plan, Tranche I participant Dr. Liu will see his annual bonus cut by US$1 million over three years in exchange for incentive options that, upon full exercise, will give him a 5% stake in PDT Xuzhou’s enlarged registered capital without upfront consideration. The grant to Dr. Liu is classified as a connected transaction subject to reporting and announcement requirements but does not require independent shareholder approval, signalling the company’s use of equity participation to retain key leadership while staying within regulatory and dilution limits.

More about IDG Energy Investment Limited

Productive Technologies Company Limited, formerly IDG Energy Investment Limited, is listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and operates through subsidiaries such as PDT Xuzhou. The group focuses on technology-related industrial operations and uses equity-based incentives to align directors, senior management and key staff with its long-term growth objectives in mainland China.

data.title: CNBM Shareholders Approve 2025 Results, Dividend Plan and Board Mandate at AGM
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from China National Building Material Co ( (HK:3323) ).

China National Building Material reported that its annual general meeting, H shareholders’ class meeting and domestic shareholders’ class meeting were duly convened in Beijing on 29 April 2026 in compliance with PRC company law and the company’s articles. All directors attended, and there were no shareholders required to abstain from voting under Hong Kong Listing Rules, underscoring orderly corporate governance.

At the AGM, shareholders or proxies representing about 65.35% of the company’s voting share capital participated, and all resolutions were passed by poll with overwhelming majorities. Investors approved the 2025 board, supervisory committee and audit reports, endorsed the 2025 profit distribution and final dividend plan, and granted the board broad authority to determine whether and how to distribute an interim dividend for 2026, clarifying the company’s capital return framework for the coming year.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3323) stock is a Buy with a HK$5.90 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China National Building Material Co stock, see the HK:3323 Stock Forecast page.

More about China National Building Material Co

China National Building Material Company Limited is a PRC-incorporated joint stock company engaged in the building materials sector. The group is listed in Hong Kong with both H shares and domestic shares, and operates under PRC company law and the Hong Kong Listing Rules, serving a broad base of institutional and retail shareholders in mainland China and overseas markets.

data.title: Sanhua Intelligent Controls Posts Strong Q1 Cash Flow Despite Softer Returns on Equity
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Zhejiang Sanhua Intelligent Controls Co., Ltd. ( (HK:2050) ) has provided an announcement.

Zhejiang Sanhua Intelligent Controls reported modest top-line growth for the first quarter of 2026, with revenue rising 1.36% year-on-year to RMB 7.77 billion, while net profit attributable to shareholders increased 2.68% to RMB 927.6 million. Profitability quality improved as net profit excluding non-recurring items jumped 15.52%, and operating cash flow surged 136.5% to RMB 1.11 billion, even as basic and diluted earnings per share declined and weighted average ROE weakened, indicating stronger cash generation but some pressure on per-share returns and overall capital efficiency.

Total assets were broadly stable at about RMB 49.36 billion, while equity attributable to shareholders rose 2.77% to RMB 32.63 billion over the period. The unaudited figures, prepared under Chinese accounting standards, suggest the group is maintaining balance sheet strength and improving underlying operations and cash flow, but faces challenges in translating earnings growth into higher returns on equity, which may be closely watched by investors assessing capital deployment and profitability trends.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2050) stock is a Buy with a HK$37.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zhejiang Sanhua Intelligent Controls Co., Ltd. stock, see the HK:2050 Stock Forecast page.

More about Zhejiang Sanhua Intelligent Controls Co., Ltd.

Zhejiang Sanhua Intelligent Controls Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated joint stock company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the intelligent controls and components sector. The group focuses on manufacturing and supplying control solutions, primarily for applications that require advanced, automated regulation of systems, serving both domestic Chinese and international markets.

China Ting Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:3398) ) has issued an announcement.

China Ting Group Holdings Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 29 May 2026 in Hangzhou, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements and the reports of directors and the auditor for the year ended 31 December 2025. The agenda underscores routine but important governance matters that help maintain transparency and regulatory compliance for investors.

Shareholders will vote on the re-election of two executive directors, Mr. Ting Man Yi and Mr. Ting Hung Yi, and independent non-executive director Ms. Li Yuet Mui, Xera, as well as authorise the board to set directors’ remuneration and re-appoint BDO Limited as auditor. The meeting will also consider granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, enhancing financial flexibility for future capital-raising while potentially diluting existing shareholders if the authority is exercised.

China Ting Group Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and traded under stock code 3398.
